data.title: Lab Session Schedule
data.content: Lab sessions will be held every Monday and Wednesday. Please check the schedule for your group.

data.title: Welcome to Cross-Platform Mobile Development!
data.content: Welcome to the course! We will be learning Flutter and Dart to build amazing mobile applications.Please review the syllabus attached below.

data.title: Lab 01: Flutter Basics
data.description: Complete the basic Flutter exercises including widget creation and state management.

data.title: Lab 02: State Management
data.description: Implement state management using Provider and Riverpod.

data.questionText: What is the DOM in web development?
data.explanation: DOM stands for Document Object Model, representing the page structure.

data.questionText: What does HTML stand for?
data.explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language.

data.questionText: Which language is used for styling web pages?
data.explanation: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) is used for styling web pages.

data.questionText: What is a RESTful API?

choices.text: An architectural style for web services

data.explanation: REST is an architectural style for designing web services.

data.questionText: What is the box model in CSS?

choices.text: How elements are structured with margin, border, padding, and content

data.explanation: The box model describes how elements are structured in CSS.

data.questionText: What is JavaScript primarily used for?
data.explanation: JavaScript is primarily used to add interactivity to web pages.

data.questionText: Which HTTP method is used to retrieve data?
data.explanation: GET method is used to retrieve data from a server.

data.questionText: What is responsive web design?

choices.text: Websites that adapt to different screen sizes

data.explanation: Responsive design ensures websites work well on different screen sizes.

data.questionText: What is the purpose of semantic HTML?

choices.text: To provide meaning to content structure

data.explanation: Semantic HTML provides meaning and structure to content.

data.questionText: What is AJAX?
data.explanation: AJAX allows asynchronous communication with servers without page reload.

data.questionText: Which method is called when a StatefulWidget is first created?
data.explanation: initState() is called once when the StatefulWidget is first created.

data.questionText: Which Flutter layout widget allows children to be positioned absolutely?
data.explanation: Stack allows children to be positioned absolutely using Positioned widgets.

data.questionText: Which programming language is used by Flutter?
data.explanation: Dart is the programming language used by Flutter.

data.questionText: What is hot reload in Flutter?

choices.text: A feature to restart the entire application
A feature to inject updated source code into the running app

data.explanation: Hot reload injects updated source code into the running app without losing state.

data.questionText: Which Flutter widget is used to display scrollable content?
data.explanation: ListView is used to display a scrollable list of widgets.

data.questionText: Which state management solution is built by the Flutter team?
data.explanation: Provider is the state management solution built by the Flutter team.

data.questionText: What does the async keyword do in Dart?

choices.text: Makes a function return a Future

data.explanation: The async keyword makes a function return a Future for asynchronous operations.

data.questionText: What is Flutter?
data.explanation: Flutter is Google's UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications.

data.questionText: What is a StatefulWidget in Flutter?

choices.text: A widget that can change its state during runtime
A widget for handling HTTP requests

data.explanation: StatefulWidget can change its state during runtime using setState().

data.questionText: What is the purpose of the BuildContext in Flutter?

choices.text: To locate widgets in the widget tree

data.explanation: BuildContext is used to locate widgets in the widget tree.

data.questionText: Which SQL command is used to retrieve data from a database?
data.explanation: SELECT is used to retrieve data from database tables.

data.questionText: Which SQL JOIN returns all records when there is a match in either table?
data.explanation: FULL OUTER JOIN returns all records when there is a match in either table.

data.questionText: What is ACID in database systems?
data.explanation: ACID stands for Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability.

data.questionText: Which SQL clause is used to filter records?
data.explanation: WHERE clause is used to filter records based on conditions.

data.questionText: Which SQL command is used to create a new table?
data.explanation: CREATE TABLE is used to create a new table in the database.

data.questionText: What does SQL stand for?
data.explanation: SQL stands for Structured Query Language.

data.questionText: What is a foreign key?

choices.text: A field that links to a primary key in another table

data.explanation: A foreign key links to a primary key in another table to establish relationships.

data.questionText: What is a primary key in a database?

choices.text: A key that opens the database
A unique identifier for each record
The first column in a table

data.explanation: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a database table.

data.questionText: What is normalization in database design?
data.explanation: Normalization organizes data to reduce redundancy and improve integrity.

data.questionText: Which type of database relationship is most common?
data.explanation: One-to-Many is the most common database relationship.

data.questionText: What is reinforcement learning?
data.explanation: Reinforcement learning involves agents learning through rewards and punishments.

data.questionText: What is deep learning?

choices.text: Machine learning with neural networks having many layers

data.explanation: Deep learning uses neural networks with multiple hidden layers.

data.questionText: What is artificial intelligence?

choices.text: Computer programs that can think like humans

data.explanation: AI refers to computer systems that can perform tasks typically requiring human intelligence.

data.questionText: What is machine learning?
data.explanation: Machine learning involves algorithms that learn patterns from data.

data.questionText: Which of the following is a supervised learning algorithm?
data.explanation: Linear regression is a supervised learning algorithm that learns from labeled data.

data.questionText: What is a neural network?

choices.text: A computing system inspired by biological neural networks

data.explanation: Neural networks are computing systems inspired by biological neural networks.

data.questionText: What is the purpose of an activation function in neural networks?
data.explanation: Activation functions introduce non-linearity to neural networks.

data.questionText: What is overfitting in machine learning?

choices.text: When a model is too big for memory
When a model performs well on training data but poorly on new data
When a model trains too fast
When a model uses too much CPU

data.explanation: Overfitting occurs when a model learns training data too well but fails to generalize.

data.questionText: What is natural language processing (NLP)?

choices.text: Teaching computers to understand human language

data.explanation: NLP involves teaching computers to understand and process human language.

data.questionText: What is the difference between AI and Machine Learning?

choices.text: AI is broader, ML is a subset of AI
ML is broader, AI is a subset of ML

data.explanation: AI is the broader field, while ML is a specific approach within AI.

data.questionText: asas
asas
